title: Improve ADV latency

tasks.title: Audit session-debt status value and choose remove-or-confine path
tasks.verification: Audit-only task; no file changes. Evidence: status.ts currently calls scanOpenCodeSessionDebt unconditionally in status assembly; status.test asserts health detail plus summary recommendation. Decision: confine OpenCode session-debt diagnostics to hygiene view only, not summary or health. Rationale: diagnostic has value for OpenCode DB/session archaeology, but user questioned summary value and it is not core Temporal/ADV health. Downstream status task must remove summary recommendation and update tests accordingly; detailed hygiene can retain it with freshness/degraded metadata if retained.
tasks.implementation_summary: Audited `scanOpenCodeSessionDebt` and status integration. Current status code calls `scanOpenCodeSessionDebt()` unconditionally while building status output and pushes a doctor recommendation into summary recommendations. Current tests assert health detail plus summary recommendation. Selected remove/confine outcome: keep diagnostic value only in hygiene view (or explicit doctor tooling), not summary or health. Downstream lazy-status task must update provider plan/tests so summary does not call session-debt and health does not pay session-debt cost; hygiene may retain it with freshness/degraded metadata.
tasks.notes: Decision: confine OpenCode session-debt diagnostics to hygiene view only; remove from summary and health. It is useful for local OpenCode DB/session archaeology, but not summary-critical and not core health for normal ADV latency path.

tasks.title: Implement lazy `adv_status` view plan and slim summary output with RED tests
tasks.verification: RED: `pnpm test -- src/tools/status.test.ts` failed with 3 expected failures for summary debt recommendation, summary detailed-provider invocation, and health session-debt exposure. GREEN: `pnpm test -- src/tools/status.test.ts` passed. Typecheck: `pnpm run typecheck` passed.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added `buildStatusViewPlan(view)` and conditional provider execution in `plugin/src/tools/status.ts`. Summary now skips queue serviceability, search attributes, worktree cleanup/census, session debt, health snapshot, external hygiene, snapshot health, peer sessions, plugin runtime, and project metadata while preserving recent-change enrichment and required context snapshots. Summary formatted detail sections are blanked. Health no longer exposes session-debt; hygiene retains session-debt. Updated status tests to assert session-debt confinement, summary non-invocation of detailed providers, slim formatted sections, and adjusted search-attribute recommendation expectations. Verification: RED status tests failed before implementation; GREEN `pnpm test -- src/tools/status.test.ts` passed; `pnpm run typecheck` passed.
tasks.notes: Implemented planned `adv_status` execution so summary skips detailed providers; summary formatted detail sections are slimmed; OpenCode session-debt is confined to hygiene and removed from health/summary hot paths.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial GREEN attempt failed: retained cleanup count expected in health, live-only debt test still expected health session-debt, search-attribute recommendation test expected summary recommendation, and typecheck required queue serviceability nullable type.

attempts.error: `pnpm test -- src/tools/status.test.ts` after RED tests failed with expected summary/session-debt assertions.
attempts.diagnosis: RED tests correctly exposed existing eager detailed-provider behavior.
attempts.fix_tried: Implemented status view plan and conditional providers.

attempts.error: Post-implementation tests/typecheck failed due test expectations and nullable queue serviceability type.
attempts.diagnosis: Health should keep worktree cleanup count but not session-debt; search-attribute recommendation belongs to health; fetchStatusQueueServiceability can return null.
attempts.fix_tried: Set health worktreeCleanup true, updated tests to hygiene/session-debt and health search-attribute recommendation, allowed null queueServiceability.

tasks.title: Add always-on latency telemetry for tools, `adv_status` phases, and `adv_run_test` substeps
tasks.verification: RED metrics tests for new tool durations + recent phase ring failed before implementation; GREEN: `pnpm test -- src/utils/metrics.test.ts src/tools/test.test.ts src/tools/status.test.ts` pass; `pnpm test -- src/utils/safe-execute.test.ts` pass; `pnpm run typecheck` passes. Wired always-on `recordToolDuration` into `safeExecute`/`safeExecuteSimple`; added `recordPhaseDuration`/`withRecordedPhase`; status phases timed (`statusLoad`, `recentChangeEnrichment`, `worktreeCleanup`, `sessionDebtScan`, `healthSnapshot`, `snapshotHealth`, `formatOutput`, `recentChangeScopeFilter`, `migrationStatus`); `adv_run_test` substeps timed (`targetRouting`, `taskLookup`, `commandExecution`, `outputShaping`).
tasks.implementation_summary: Extended `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts` with `adv_tool_durations` (count/total/last/max/error_count per tool) and a bounded `recent_phase_durations` ring (cap 50) plus `recordToolDuration`, `recordPhaseDuration`, and `withRecordedPhase`. Wired always-on `recordToolDuration` into `safeExecute` and `safeExecuteSimple` for both success and error paths in `plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts`; kept `ADV_PROFILE` file logging as an opt-in extra. Wrapped expensive `adv_status` phases (statusLoad, migrationStatus, recentChangeScopeFilter, recentChangeEnrichment, worktreeCleanup, sessionDebtScan, healthSnapshot, snapshotHealth, formatOutput) in `withRecordedPhase`. Wrapped `adv_run_test` substeps (`targetRouting`, `taskLookup`, `commandExecution`, `outputShaping`) including success/error outcome for command execution. Added metrics tests for new duration/phase APIs; added status health-view test asserting `adv_status` phase names recorded; added `adv_run_test` substep telemetry tests for both success and non-zero exit.
tasks.notes: Telemetry surface added: always-on per-tool durations, status phase timings, adv_run_test substep timings.

error_recovery.last_error: typecheck failure: args.target_path narrowing lost inside async closure.

attempts.error: src/tools/test.ts(221,17): Type 'string | undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'.
attempts.diagnosis: Closure passed to withRecordedPhase loses the outer narrowing on `args.target_path`.
attempts.fix_tried: Captured `args.target_path` into a local `const targetPath` before the closure.

tasks.title: Profile and safely optimize `adv_run_test` hot path with compatibility matrix
tasks.verification: Added shell-compatibility matrix tests for `adv_run_test` covering pipe/metacharacter semantics, task-missing short-circuit, SIGTERM timeout classification, max-buffer classification, non-zero exit semantics, and per-call fresh-execution. `pnpm test -- src/tools/test.test.ts` passes. No subprocess implementation change — exec retained per KD-8 because compatibility evidence (and design KD-8 default) requires preserved shell semantics and profiling did not reveal a safe wrapper-overhead win to justify a runner change at this time.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added a shell-compatibility test matrix in `plugin/src/tools/test.test.ts` covering: pipe/metacharacter execution through the shell (`printf | wc -l` → exit 0, output 3), task-missing short-circuit that prevents subprocess execution, SIGTERM-driven timeout classification (timedOut true, diagnostic prefix, timeoutMs surfaced), max-buffer classification on overflow (maxBufferExceeded true, diagnostic prefix), per-call fresh execution (no caching; two consecutive calls produce different outputs and both invoke task lookup), and non-zero exit reporting without false timeout/maxBuffer flags. Subprocess implementation retained per KD-8: `child_process.exec` keeps exact shell semantics for the existing `command: string` contract, and profiling did not reveal a safe wrapper-overhead win to justify a runner change. Telemetry substeps from the previous task (`targetRouting`, `taskLookup`, `commandExecution`, `outputShaping`) remain wired, including success/error outcome for `commandExecution`.
tasks.notes: Compatibility matrix verified; semantics preserved; substep telemetry exists. Subprocess implementation unchanged per KD-8.

tasks.title: Align Temporal Visibility project-scope queries for summary-read completeness
tasks.verification: Switched `list-change-workflows.buildVisibilityQuery` from unregistered `AdvProjectId` to registered `AdvAffectedProjects` (KeywordList equality matches single project membership). Updated tests assert the new attribute and explicitly reject `AdvProjectId`. Removed stale drift note from `visibility-claim-queries.ts`. `pnpm test -- src/temporal/list-change-workflows.test.ts src/temporal/visibility-claim-queries.test.ts` passes; `pnpm run typecheck` passes.
tasks.implementation_summary: `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts:buildVisibilityQuery` now scopes change-workflow enumeration by `AdvAffectedProjects` (registered KeywordList in `ADV_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES`) instead of the unregistered `AdvProjectId` Keyword. KeywordList equality matches when the project ID is a member of the list, so the change preserves single-project and multi-project change workflows. `plugin/src/temporal/visibility-claim-queries.ts` already used `AdvAffectedProjects`; stale drift note removed. List-change-workflows unit tests updated to assert the new attribute and to reject `AdvProjectId`. Verification: targeted vitest run for list-change-workflows + visibility-claim-queries passes; `pnpm run typecheck` passes. KD-6: this is the "converge on registered AdvAffectedProjects" path; legacy `AdvProjectId` is left only in unused contract metadata (`contracts.ts:ADVANCE_TEMPORAL_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES`) and observability mapping references, neither of which drives Visibility queries.
tasks.notes: Visibility list query converged on registered `AdvAffectedProjects` KeywordList, matching claim-coordination scope.

tasks.title: Implement correctness-safe summary read model for default list/status paths
tasks.verification: Added optional `Store.changes.listSummary` and Temporal-store implementation that serves memo/cache hits without per-change full hydration; falls back to `listResolvedChanges`/`getTemporalChange` for misses and uses the full path for archived/closed/content-filter callers. Wired `adv_change_list` to prefer `listSummary` when available. New `changes.test.ts` cases prove (a) memo-only candidates skip hydration entirely, (b) cache-served candidates skip hydration, and (c) archived/closed callers route through the authoritative `listResolvedChanges` path. `pnpm test -- src/tools/change.test.ts src/storage/store-temporal/changes.test.ts src/storage/store-temporal/index.test.ts` passes; `pnpm run typecheck` passes.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added optional `Store.changes.listSummary` to `plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts` with a documented surface that mirrors `list()` but reports `hydrationStats` so telemetry callers can observe memo/cache/hydration counts. Implemented in `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts`: when archived/closed inclusion or content-search filters are requested, the method defers to the authoritative `listResolvedChanges` path; otherwise it unions candidate IDs from `ChangeSummaryMemo`, Visibility (when `bundle.client.workflow.list` exists), and disk `listChangeDirs`, then resolves each id via cache → memo → `getTemporalChange` fallback. Default `adv_change_list` (`plugin/src/tools/change.ts`) prefers `listSummary` when present, preserving filter semantics and recency enrichment without changing the response shape. New `changes.test.ts` cases prove memo-only candidates skip hydration, cache-served candidates skip hydration, and archived/closed callers route through the authoritative `listResolvedChanges` path. Targeted vitest run for change tool + store-temporal modules and full typecheck pass.
tasks.notes: Added optional listSummary surface; default adv_change_list wired through it; tests prove memo/cache short-circuits + authoritative fallback for terminal/content filters.

error_recovery.last_error: test seeding for cache fallback initially required memo seed to surface the id

attempts.error: fromCache assertion failed because the diskOnlyChange id was never added to the candidate set after memo.invalidate.
attempts.diagnosis: listSummary candidate set comes from memo/visibility/disk; pre-populating only changeCache without memo means the id never enters the loop.
attempts.fix_tried: Kept the memo seed in place (it dictates the candidate set) and used it as the entry point while the changeCache short-circuit served the row.

tasks.title: Repair and document latency benchmark harness with status/list/show/test samples
tasks.verification: Benchmark harness rewritten as documented isolated substitute backed by `createDiskStore` (rq-advLatencyBench01) plus opt-in `--mode temporal` that intentionally refuses to fabricate a Temporal bundle. Smoke run completed: `pnpm exec tsx scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts --repo-root <worktree> --change-id improveAdvLatency --iterations 3 --warmup 1 --out reports/latency-disk-sample.md` produced summary ≈55ms vs health ≈113ms, confirming the lazy view plan's measurable win. New `docs/bench-adv-latency.md` documents command, fixture shape, output location, and the manual-only nature of the bench. `pnpm run typecheck` passes.
tasks.implementation_summary: Rewrote `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts` to default to a documented isolated substitute (`createDiskStore`) so the harness initializes under the Temporal-only `createStore` contract without a live worker. Added `--mode disk|temporal`, fixture metadata (mode, substitute, task id used, ADV_PROFILE), and timed samples for `adv_status view:"summary"`, `adv_status view:"health"`, `adv_change_list`, `adv_change_show`, `store.tasks.list` (disk fallback because `adv_task_list` requires Temporal), and `adv_run_test echo`/`true`. `--mode temporal` intentionally refuses to fabricate a TemporalClientBundle so results are never silently substituted. Added `docs/bench-adv-latency.md` documenting command, fixture shape, output location, and manual-only nature. Added `plugin/.gitignore` so `reports/` artifacts stay out of git. Sample run captured wins: summary ≈55ms vs health ≈113ms = ~2× from lazy view planning. Typecheck passes.
tasks.notes: Benchmark rebuilt; manual disk substitute + opt-in temporal mode; doc + sample report shape verified.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial bench tried to run adv_task_list which requires Temporal handle.

attempts.error: adv_task_list throws `Temporal service not available` under the disk substitute.
attempts.diagnosis: adv_task_list is a Temporal-required tool; the disk store has no workflow handle.
attempts.fix_tried: Replaced adv_task_list with `store.tasks.list` for disk mode; reserved adv_task_list/show for the temporal-mode harness path.

tasks.title: Add spec deltas and requirement citations for latency/read/test contracts
tasks.verification: Added six new requirements to `.adv/specs/advance-meta` (version bumped to 1.12.0): rq-advStatusLazyView01, rq-changeSummaryReadModel01, rq-advLatencyTelemetry01, rq-advLatencyBench01, rq-visibilityProjectScope01, rq-advRunTestLatency01. JSON parses cleanly (`node -e JSON.parse(...)`). Added in-code citations referencing the new requirement IDs in `plugin/src/tools/status.ts`, `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts`, `plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts`, `plugin/src/tools/test.ts`, `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts`, and `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts`. `pnpm run typecheck` passes.
tasks.implementation_summary: Extended `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json` (version 1.11.0 → 1.12.0) with `rq-advStatusLazyView01`, `rq-changeSummaryReadModel01`, `rq-advLatencyTelemetry01`, `rq-advLatencyBench01`, `rq-visibilityProjectScope01`, and `rq-advRunTestLatency01`. Each requirement carries Given/When/Then scenarios anchored to the implementation surfaces (status view planning, listSummary contract, in-memory telemetry, manual bench harness, Visibility attribute, adv_run_test substep telemetry). Added in-code citations referencing the new IDs and version in `plugin/src/tools/status.ts:buildStatusViewPlan`, `plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:recordToolDuration`, `plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts:listSummary`, `plugin/src/tools/test.ts`, `plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts:buildVisibilityQuery`, and `plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts`. JSON validity verified via `node -e JSON.parse`; `pnpm run typecheck` passes.
tasks.notes: Added six rq-* deltas to advance-meta (v1.12) and citation comments to implementing modules.

tasks.title: Run contract-wide verification, structural CI, and manual latency benchmark evidence
tasks.verification: Global verification: `pnpm run check` passes (typecheck + lint + format) after fixing unused-var lint and prettier whitespace; `pnpm test` full suite passes; manual latency benchmark captured at plugin/reports/latency-disk-final.md (summary p50 ≈41.7ms vs health p50 ≈114.6ms = ~2.7× warm-default win). `adv_contract_review_matrix_set` recorded 25/25 rows (AC1-AC11 pass, C1-C8 respected, DONT1-DONT6 respected). `adv_change_validate strict:true` passes with only the expected NO_DELTAS warning.
tasks.implementation_summary: Closed out the change-wide verification cycle: ran `pnpm run check` (typecheck + isolation + lockfile policy + lint + format:check) and `pnpm test` (full suite); both pass. Fixed an unused `ops` lint regression and reformatted touched files via `pnpm run format`. Captured manual latency evidence via the rewritten benchmark (`plugin/reports/latency-disk-final.md`, 8 iterations, warmup 2): `adv_status view:"summary"` p50 ≈41.7ms vs `view:"health"` p50 ≈114.6ms = ~2.7× faster warm-default read. Recorded a full contract review matrix (25 rows) via `adv_contract_review_matrix_set` — AC1-AC11 marked pass, C1-C8 respected, DONT1-DONT6 respected, each row carrying source/test/file evidence. Final `adv_change_validate strict:true` passes with only the documented NO_DELTAS warning (deltas live in the spec file, not the change.json deltas surface).
tasks.notes: Repo check and full vitest pass; manual latency report captured; contract review matrix recorded with 25/25 rows passing.

subagent_reports.topic: Opportunity scout — missed leverage points within approved improveAdvLatency scope

sources.label: Approved proposal artifact (improveAdvLatency)
sources.summary: Confirms in-scope: lazy adv_status views, summary read model for list/status, latency telemetry, lightweight adv_change_show, spec deltas where required; out-of-scope: replacing Temporal, weakening validation/gate semantics.

sources.label: Repo-improve prep evidence pack
sources.summary: Top severity-HIGH findings: full status output built unconditionally; status invokes advWorktreeCleanup on every call; list paths hydrate full changes after ID union; latency telemetry exists but `recordWallTimeMs` never called from production hook.

sources.label: applyStatusView in tools/status.ts
sources.summary: Comment at L913-916 explicitly states `The full output is built unconditionally` and `applyStatusView` is post-hoc projection. Lines 1093-1471 unconditionally fetch Temporal health, queue serviceability, search attributes, project config (incl. featureFlagSources), recent-change enrichment (per-change store.changes.get + proposal read + snapshot/ticker + dependency status + clarify checks + recency rec), advWorktreeCleanup, worktree census, OpenCode session debt scan, health snapshot, snapshot health, specs recount, peer sessions, plugin runtime, project metadata, then formatStatusOutput, before applyStatusView at 1531.

sources.label: Status worktree-cleanup coupling
sources.summary: advWorktreeCleanup("status", ...) runs on every adv_status call, including summary view. Wrapped in best-effort try/catch but still pays initWorktreeStateDb + getPendingDeletes I/O on the hot read path.

sources.label: List read-path hydration
sources.summary: listResolvedChanges unions memo/visibility/disk(/archive) IDs then per-ID calls getTemporalChange (or disk fallback) in batches of 20 — full hydration, even when caller only needs summary fields.

sources.label: ChangeSummaryMemo
sources.summary: In-process Map<changeId, ChangeSummary> with id/title/status/gateProgress/taskCounts/lastActivityAt. Comment at L583-586 explicitly says memo is `used as a cache within per-change hydration, not as a completeness authority` — never serves a summary-list response on its own.

sources.label: Visibility attribute drift
sources.summary: buildVisibilityQuery filters on `AdvProjectId` (unregistered per local comment); backlog claim queries filter on the registered `AdvAffectedProjects`. Inconsistency acknowledged in code as pre-existing; risk of silent fallback to disk if registration drifts.

sources.label: Latency telemetry gap
sources.locator: plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:85 + plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts:380-449 + plugin/src/index.ts:819-867 + lgrep search for recordWallTimeMs
sources.summary: recordWallTimeMs defined and tested but NOT invoked outside tests — production tool.execute.after only calls recordAdvToolCall. ADV_PROFILE=1 records per-tool wall time to a file log via recordToolProfile, but no in-process per-tool or per-substep counters are surfaced to view:"health".

sources.label: Bench harness broken
sources.summary: bench-adv-latency calls createStore(repoRoot) without temporalBundle; store.ts requires temporalBundle since Temporal-only cutover. Bench cannot run today — performance budgets/regression coverage rely on this script.

sources.label: Probe-cache LBP foundation
sources.summary: Existing lru-cache TTL+allowStale+allowStaleOnFetchAbort/Rejection wrapper used by Temporal/queue/search-attribute/worktree-census/snapshot probes. Drop-in pattern for any new cached substep added in this change.

sources.label: advance-meta rq-statusProbeCache01
sources.summary: Mandates bounded cached probes for health/recovery, _freshness metadata, and that stale data must not authorize safety-critical mutations. Any new summary-index/cache must respect this.

sources.label: Temporal Visibility/Search-Attributes docs
sources.locator: Context7 /temporalio/sdk-typescript — Search Attributes and Visibility in TypeScript
sources.summary: Confirms client.workflow.list({ query }) is the canonical filter-by-search-attribute API and client.workflow.count(query) returns a Count result. Validates Visibility-first list path and supports adding count() for cheap status size signals instead of hydrating.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Approved scope targets four levers: (1) lazy view planning in adv_status, (2) summary-only read model for list/status defaults, (3) production-visible latency telemetry, (4) lightweight adv_change_show. Current implementation builds the full status payload before projection, runs worktree cleanup I/O on every status call, hydrates full Change objects on every list call, and never surfaces per-tool/per-substep wall-clock metrics in production. Probe-cache pattern, ChangeSummaryMemo, and Temporal Visibility APIs already exist as the foundation for the durable fix — most opportunities are wiring, not new infrastructure. Visibility attribute drift (AdvProjectId vs AdvAffectedProjects) is an adjacent correctness/perf risk explicitly in scope-relevant for the summary read model.

validation.notes: All five candidates are evidence-cited, contract-tied to the approved proposal, and respect Temporal/gate/archive safety. None require user-value tradeoffs; visibility attribute alignment surfaces to user only because it touches search-attribute registration.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt 5 ScoutCandidates inside discovery agenda. Auto-adopt SC-1 (lazy adv_status view planning) and SC-2 (defer worktree cleanup out of status hot path) — both are pure execution-plan refactors with strong existing safety rails and no user-visible contract change beyond what the approved proposal already authorizes. Surface SC-3 (promote ChangeSummaryMemo into a contract-bound summary read model for adv_change_list/adv_status defaults) to user — needs user confirmation on which default fields are compatibility-sensitive and may need a spec delta in advance-meta. Surface SC-4 (visibility attribute alignment: AdvProjectId → AdvAffectedProjects or register AdvProjectId) to user — touches search-attribute registration, has migration implications, but is on the critical path for a reliable Visibility-first summary model. Auto-adopt SC-5 (wire recordWallTimeMs + per-substep timing into tool.execute.after and view:"health") — telemetry-only, no behavior change. Repair bench-adv-latency.ts to unblock regression evidence (small fix, in scope).
follow_ups: ScoutCandidate SC-1: id=lazy-status-view-plan, title="Make adv_status view-lazy via execution planning", opportunity="Move from post-hoc applyStatusView projection to per-view execution plan — summary view computes only specs.count, recent IDs+titles+recency, recommendations, temporal_health_ok bit, worktree count; defers temporal queue serviceability, search-attribute probe, opencodeSessionDebt scan, healthSnapshot, snapshotHealth, externalStateHygiene, project_metadata, plugin_runtime, peerSessions, featureFlagSources, autoManagedCensus into health/hygiene views", evidence="plugin/src/tools/status.ts:911-916 + 1093-1531 + docs/repo-improve-prep.md:35-40", contract_tie="Proposal Success Criteria #1 (lazy summary view)", payoff="Eliminates >10 substeps and >1 Temporal probe + project_metadata read + peer-session scan + plugin_runtime resolution from default summary; expected dominant gain on cold-start orientation calls", risk="LOW — applyStatusView already proves the projection contract; need compatibility tests that summary view still includes all rq-advStatusView01 summary fields", fate="adopt_now", routing="discovery/design task list"

ScoutCandidate SC-2: id=defer-status-worktree-cleanup, title="Move worktree cleanup discovery out of adv_status default path", opportunity="Keep advWorktreeCleanup(\"status\", ...) + initWorktreeStateDb + getPendingDeletes only in view:\"hygiene\" or as explicit adv_worktree_cleanup invocation; cache terminalCleanupRetained via probe-cache TTL when summary needs the count", evidence="plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1274-1300 + docs/repo-improve-prep.md:42-47", contract_tie="Proposal Success Criteria #1 (lazy view), Out-of-Scope avoidance: 'Must not create hidden background mutations from read-only tools'", payoff="Removes worktree-state DB open + cleanup retry I/O from every status call; eliminates a known amplifier when worktree state is large or poisoned", risk="LOW — cleanup retry already best-effort and TTL-cacheable; existing adv_worktree_cleanup tool provides explicit recovery path", fate="adopt_now", routing="discovery/design task list"

ScoutCandidate SC-3: id=summary-index-read-model, title="Promote ChangeSummaryMemo to a contract-bound summary read model for list/status defaults", opportunity="Define a typed ChangeSummary projection (id, title, status, gateProgress, taskCounts, lastActivityAt, fast_follow_of, recency) populated by workflow signals + Visibility annotations and served directly to adv_change_list default + adv_status recent-change enrichment without per-change store.changes.get / proposal read / context snapshot. Full hydration only when caller passes status filter requiring detail, explicit include, or view:\"changes\".", evidence="plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts:575-740, plugin/src/storage/store-temporal-memo.ts:1-115, plugin/src/tools/status.ts:1243-1272 (single-pass per-change enrichment with snapshot/proposal/dependency/clarify checks), plugin/src/tools/change.ts:1702 (adv_change_list)", contract_tie="Proposal Success Criteria #2 (avoid per-change full hydration when summary satisfies contract); Constraint: stale summary data must not authorize gates/archive (advance-meta rq-statusProbeCache01)", payoff="Removes O(N) full-Change hydration + O(N) proposal-file reads + O(N) snapshot builds + O(N) clarify-readiness runs from default list/status. Largest expected gain at >10 changes.", risk="MEDIUM — requires correctness-safe invalidation tied to existing fireSignalAndRefresh discipline (rq-cacheRefresh01); needs compatibility audit of fields callers expect on default; may need an advance-meta spec delta to formally separate summary vs detail contract. User input needed on which default fields are non-negotiable vs movable behind include flags.", fate="surface_to_user", routing="discovery clarification (user-facing) + design proposal for summary contract"

ScoutCandidate SC-4: id=visibility-attribute-alignment, title="Align project-scope Visibility attribute (AdvProjectId vs AdvAffectedProjects) for reliable Visibility-first reads", opportunity="Pick one of: (a) register AdvProjectId as Keyword in ADV_SEARCH_ATTRIBUTES so listChangeWorkflowIds keeps its current query; or (b) migrate listChangeWorkflowIds to AdvAffectedProjects contains_any clause so it matches backlog-claim queries; remove inconsistent pre-existing comment.", evidence="plugin/src/temporal/visibility-claim-queries.ts:11-15 (explicit local comment naming AdvProjectId as pre-existing inconsistency); plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts:24-27, 83-84", contract_tie="Indirect: the summary-index read model (SC-3) depends on Visibility being a structurally reliable enumeration source; misaligned attributes silently fall back to disk-listing, defeating the perf win. Proposal Failure Handling: 'Lazy view planning must fail closed for correctness-sensitive data'.", payoff="Eliminates a class of silent slow-path fallbacks; reduces operator confusion ('why is list slow/offline?'); precondition for SC-3 paying off", risk="MEDIUM — touches Temporal search-attribute registration / namespace metadata; needs migration plan and tests; user input warranted because choice (a) vs (b) has spec implications and rollout sequencing affects existing deployments", fate="surface_to_user", routing="discovery clarification (user-facing) — pick alignment direction before design lock"

ScoutCandidate SC-5: id=production-latency-telemetry, title="Wire per-tool wall_time_ms and adv_status substep timings into production telemetry + repair bench harness", opportunity="Three-part bundle: (1) call recordWallTimeMs from tool.execute.after with performance.now() delta around tool execution; (2) add a lightweight named-span helper in adv_status that records per-substep durations (e.g. status_load, recent_change_enrichment, temporal_health_probe, worktree_cleanup, snapshot_health) and surfaces them in view:\"health\" alongside existing counters from getMetrics(); (3) fix bench-adv-latency.ts to construct the required temporalBundle (or guard with ADV_DISABLE_TEMPORAL path) so regression budgets can run.", evidence="plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts:85 + plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts:380-449 + plugin/src/index.ts:819-867 (recordWallTimeMs only referenced from tests); plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts:44 vs plugin/src/storage/store.ts:46-49 ('temporalBundle is required'); docs/repo-improve-prep.md:67-72", contract_tie="Proposal Success Criteria #3 (per-tool duration_ms + named adv_status phase durations) and #4 (regression coverage / documented benchmark workflow)", payoff="First-class diagnosis of slow tools/substeps; unblocks any future budget assertion; bench script becomes usable for before/after evidence on SC-1/SC-2/SC-3", risk="LOW — telemetry-only; counters reset on plugin init (no persistence concerns); bench fix is local. Must respect ADV_PROFILE existing log path to avoid duplicate emission.", fate="adopt_now", routing="discovery/design task list"
Warning: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN packet anchors were present and used; no missing-anchor degradation applied.

subagent_reports.topic: Design leverage points for ADV latency improvement

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.ts §applyStatusView (lines 911-940)
sources.summary: Comment confirms full output is built unconditionally; applyStatusView is post-hoc projection — KD-1 thesis validated by current code.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.ts (lines 1283, 1307)
sources.summary: Status executes advWorktreeCleanup('status') and scanOpenCodeSessionDebt() unconditionally for every adv_status call, even summary view.

sources.label: plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts §recordToolProfile (lines 380-400)
sources.summary: Per-tool duration_ms is already captured via appendProfileLog when ADV_PROFILE=1. Gated, write-only to file, not in-memory ring.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal-memo.ts §ChangeSummaryMemo
sources.summary: Existing Map<changeId, ChangeSummary> with title/status/gateProgress/taskCounts/lastActivityAt — already invalidate-on-mutate. No new memo class needed.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts §emitChangeSummarySignal (lines 269-274)
sources.summary: emitChangeSummarySignal is a NO-OP since projectWorkflow retirement. Design KD references it as live mechanism — drift.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts §listResolvedChanges (lines 575-700)
sources.summary: Always unions memo+visibility+disk IDs then batched per-change getTemporalChange. Default-path summary callers still pay full hydration cost.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts vs visibility-claim-queries.ts
sources.summary: Confirmed drift: list-change-workflows uses AdvProjectId; visibility-claim-queries uses AdvAffectedProjects. KD-6 valid prerequisite.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts §list (lines 201-269)
sources.summary: Returns summary-shaped rows (id, title, status, created_at, lastActivityAt, taskCount, completedTasks). Already a summary projection at API surface, but resolves full Change[] internally.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is mostly correct. Five leverage points found: (1) Existing safeExecute profiling hook makes a separate AC6 timer wrapper unnecessary — convert ADV_PROFILE-gated file log to always-on in-memory ring + flag-gated file log; halves telemetry implementation. (2) ChangeSummaryMemo already exists with right shape; KD-4 'new listSummary method' should be 'memo-served list path on store.changes.list when no filter forces full hydration' — avoids new public surface. (3) emitChangeSummarySignal is already a no-op; KD-5 invalidation-via-existing-mutation-paths is fine, but design language should drop the dead signal reference. (4) advWorktreeCleanup('status') and scanOpenCodeSessionDebt are unconditional in adv_status — these are the dominant tax on summary view; KD-3 is right but should be 'remove from summary path entirely' not 'audit'. (5) Visibility attribute drift (KD-6) is real and must land BEFORE memo-served summary if memo cold-starts ever rely on visibility for hydration; otherwise non-blocking for KD-1/2/3.

validation.notes: No safety or contract blockers. All five candidates preserve specs/Temporal/cache/gates/archive/worktree correctness. Summary advisory-only constraint (KD-5) already met by changes.list shape.

subagent_reports.recommendation:
ScoutCandidate rows (≤5):

1) id=SC-1 title='Reuse safeExecute profiling for telemetry' opportunity='Convert recordToolProfile from ADV_PROFILE-gated file-log to always-on in-memory ring buffer + optional file log; expose ring in adv_status view:health.' evidence='safe-execute.ts:380-400 + metrics.ts already in-memory singleton pattern' contract_tie='rq-advLatencyTelemetry01' payoff='Eliminates separate timer wrapper; one code path; production-visible without env flag' risk='LOW: bounded ring (e.g. last 200 entries), no hot-path allocation beyond what profiling already does' fate='AUTO-ADOPT' routing='execution'

2) id=SC-2 title='Memo-served changes.list fast path' opportunity='When filter has no prefix/titleContains/timestamp/status-archived-or-closed, serve list from ChangeSummaryMemo.getAll() directly, skipping listResolvedChanges N hydrations.' evidence='changes.ts:201-269 returns summary shape; memo already holds all required fields (id,title,status,taskCounts,lastActivityAt via overlay)' contract_tie='rq-changeSummaryReadModel01' payoff='Replaces N per-change getTemporalChange with O(1) Map iteration on default warm path; no new public method' risk='LOW: full fallback when filter present; safety paths (gate, archive, claims) still use changes.get/full state' fate='AUTO-ADOPT' routing='execution'

3) id=SC-3 title='Remove worktree-cleanup + session-debt from summary view' opportunity='Drop advWorktreeCleanup("status") and scanOpenCodeSessionDebt() from adv_status summary plan; keep in hygiene/health only.' evidence='status.ts:1283 + 1307 unconditional; AC2 explicitly forbids; KD-3 says audit but evidence is clear' contract_tie='rq-advStatusLazyView01 (AC2)' payoff='Eliminates the two dominant non-Temporal taxes from default warm read; matches contract' risk='LOW: hygiene/health views still surface them; no consumer of summary depends on session_debt residues per current contract' fate='AUTO-ADOPT' routing='execution'

4) id=SC-4 title='Drop dead emitChangeSummarySignal references from design' opportunity='Design KD-5/implementation-strategy step 5 cite emitChangeSummarySignal as a live invalidation mechanism; it is a no-op since projectWorkflow retired.' evidence='store-temporal/index.ts:269-274 is empty body with retirement comment' contract_tie='design-doc-correctness (no rq tie)' payoff='Prevents implementer from wiring against dead surface; saves a debugging cycle' risk='NONE: doc-only update' fate='SURFACE-TO-USER' routing='design'

5) id=SC-5 title='Sequence KD-6 visibility-attribute alignment before memo fast path' opportunity='If SC-2 memo cold-start ever falls back to visibility for hydration on new sessions, AdvProjectId vs AdvAffectedProjects drift can silently omit changes. Land KD-6 register/migrate FIRST or pin memo fallback to disk scan only.' evidence='list-change-workflows.ts:84 uses AdvProjectId; visibility-claim-queries.ts:87 uses AdvAffectedProjects' contract_tie='rq-visibilityProjectScope01' payoff='Prevents silent summary miss on cold session; preserves correctness' risk='MEDIUM if not sequenced: cold-session summary may miss recent changes' fate='SURFACE-TO-USER (sequencing decision)' routing='planning'
follow_ups: Confirm with user whether SC-3 should fully remove session-debt scan from summary path or keep behind an explicit opt-in flag (current contract says remove)
If SC-1 adopted, agree ring buffer size (recommend 200 recent invocations) and whether to keep ADV_PROFILE file-log path as opt-in extra

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.ts applyStatusView (lines 907-979)
sources.summary: Current code confirms KD-1 premise: full output built unconditionally, view applied post-hoc. Source comment explicitly says 'The full output is built unconditionally.' Design's plan-before-execute is the structural fix.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal-memo.ts ChangeSummaryMemo
sources.summary: ChangeSummaryMemo already exists as in-process Map, invalidated on mutation signals, stale-safe by design. Supports KD-4 listSummary fast path without inventing new caching layer.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/list-change-workflows.ts buildVisibilityQuery + plugin/src/temporal/visibility-claim-queries.ts header
sources.summary: Documented inconsistency: list-change-workflows.ts queries AdvProjectId; backlog-claim uses AdvAffectedProjects (registered KeywordList). Comment in visibility-claim-queries.ts explicitly flags the pre-existing inconsistency and logs it as agenda follow-up. KD-6 sequencing alignment is correct.

sources.label: plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts recordToolProfile (lines 380-399)
sources.summary: safeExecute already computes duration_ms and writes profile log when ADV_PROFILE=1. Design's 'always record in-memory, gate disk write' is a minimal extension, not a new path. Consistent with KD-7 telemetry.

sources.label: plugin/src/utils/metrics.ts AdvMetricsCounters (rq-advMetricsBaseline01)
sources.summary: Metrics singleton already counts adv_tool_calls and per-name breakdown. Extending with per-tool {count,total_ms,last_ms,max_ms} fits existing pattern. No new lifecycle invented.

sources.label: spec advance-meta rq-statusProbeCache01
sources.summary: Existing spec: stale probe data must not authorize worker-lock reclaim, restart success, override, unlock, or archive. KD-5 (summary advisory only) and DONT2 (no cache as gate/archive/claim authority) are aligned with existing law.

sources.label: spec chat-output-display rq-ctxsnap2, rq-ctxticker2.5
sources.summary: adv_status MUST emit context snapshot (rq-ctxsnap2.4) and full-box for primary change + ticker for non-primary (rq-ctxticker2.5). Design must preserve these in summary view; slim summary cannot drop _contextSnapshot. Risk: design.md does not explicitly mention preserving _contextSnapshot in summary formatted output.

sources.label: Temporal TypeScript SDK Visibility / search attributes
sources.summary: client.workflow.list({ query }) with custom search attributes is the canonical Visibility pattern; pagination is iterator-based. Design's Visibility union approach matches official guidance.

sources.label: lru-cache fetch / stale-on-error
sources.summary: lru-cache.fetch() coalesces concurrent fetches and supports stale-on-rejection — exact behavior needed for the probe-cache pattern referenced in agreement LBP analysis.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design is structurally sound and grounded in the existing codebase. Two-plane read architecture (plan-before-execute + summary read model) is the by-the-book fix for the documented post-hoc projection waste in applyStatusView. ChangeSummaryMemo + Visibility + disk-ID union is the standard Temporal-native pattern; KD-6 correctly sequences Visibility search-attribute alignment (AdvProjectId vs AdvAffectedProjects — documented inconsistency in source) before relying on Visibility as a completeness authority. Telemetry extension is minimal — uses existing safeExecute timing and existing metrics singleton (rq-advMetricsBaseline01); no new lifecycle. Safety story is strong: KD-5 + DONT2 + AC6 keep summary/cache advisory-only and authoritative paths intact for gates/archive/claims/recovery, which aligns with existing law rq-statusProbeCache01. No spec-law conflicts found. Simpler alternatives were considered (e.g., pure cache TTL) and rejected because they would still execute detailed providers — only execution planning solves the lazy-provider acceptance criterion AC1.

validation.notes: Three cautions, all minor and addressable in planning rather than design rework: (1) chat-output-display spec rq-ctxsnap2.4 + rq-ctxticker2.5 require _contextSnapshot in adv_status output; design.md does not explicitly call out preserving _contextSnapshot when slimming summary formatted output. Recommend explicit note in design that summary view still emits _contextSnapshot per spec. (2) KD-3 session-debt 'audit during implementation' is loosely scoped — acceptance criterion AC3 requires the audit conclusion; recommend planning include a defined audit checkpoint with a binary remove/confine decision before Phase A ships. (3) KD-6 Visibility attribute alignment is correctly identified as a prerequisite but is broad — design should clarify whether AdvProjectId is migrated to AdvAffectedProjects or both are supported during transition, since this affects backlog-coordination spec rq-backlogCoord01..07 and an existing agenda follow-up.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with caution. Design correctly addresses agreement objectives, respects all constraints (Temporal/Zod/gates preserved), and matches Temporal-native LBP. Before planning gate completion: (a) add a line to design.md explicitly preserving _contextSnapshot emission in summary view per chat-output-display spec; (b) commit KD-3 session-debt audit to a planning checkpoint with binary remove/confine outcome before Phase A code ships; (c) clarify KD-6 Visibility-attribute migration direction (rename AdvProjectId callers to AdvAffectedProjects, or dual-read during transition). No architectural rework required; cautions are scope/clarification not correctness defects.
follow_ups: Cross-check with backlog-coordination spec rq-backlogCoord01..07 during planning to ensure Visibility-attribute alignment does not regress claim queries.
Confirm benchmark fixture under Temporal-only contract uses isolated namespace per docs/temporal-recovery.md.
During Phase D implementation, verify ChangeSummary shape (no sourceVersion, per existing PSW-retired test in store-temporal-memo.test.ts) remains stable when adding new summary fields.

sources.label: Node.js child_process docs (Context7 /nodejs/node)
sources.summary: child_process.exec(command, opts, cb) spawns shell, buffers stdout/stderr, supports timeout (sends killSignal default SIGTERM), maxBuffer (default 1MB; exceeding terminates child + truncates output), error.code = exit code, error.signal = terminating signal. execFile/spawn default shell:false. Confirms design KD-8 'exec-default, spawn only with proven shell preservation'.

sources.label: Spec chat-output-display rq-ctxsnap2 / rq-ctxticker2.5
sources.summary: adv_status MUST emit full-box _contextSnapshot for primary active/draft/pending change and ticker for non-primary. Design KD-2 and Architecture point 1 explicitly preserve this in slim summary.

sources.label: Spec backlog-coordination rq-backlogCoord01/05
sources.summary: Backlog-claim Visibility queries use AdvAffectedProjects + AdvBacklogIssueNumber + AdvChangeStatus. Design KD-6 explicitly converges project-scoped filtering on registered AdvAffectedProjects matching this precedent; AdvProjectId remains transitional with explicit registration/documentation requirement.

sources.label: Local code: plugin/src/tools/status.ts:applyStatusView (symbol)
sources.summary: Current post-hoc projection point. Design replaces with buildStatusViewPlan(view) as primary control. Refactor target exists as described.

sources.label: Local code: plugin/src/utils/opencode-session-debt.ts:scanOpenCodeSessionDebt
sources.summary: Session-debt provider exists; KD-3 binary remove/confine decision aligns with AC3 and DONT3.

sources.label: Local code: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal-memo.ts:ChangeSummaryMemo
sources.summary: Summary memo class exists for KD-4/KD-5 reuse. KD-5 enforces advisory-only authority matching DONT2.

sources.label: Local code: plugin/src/utils/safe-execute.ts:safeExecute
sources.summary: Existing duration_ms timing infrastructure underpins telemetry plane (Phase B). Always-record proposal preserves ADV_PROFILE gating.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Revised design is internally coherent and addresses all prior validator cautions. Three-plane separation (status plan / summary read model / adv_run_test) cleanly maps to acceptance criteria: AC1-AC3 → plane 1, AC4-AC5 → plane 2, AC6 → plane 3, AC8-AC10 → telemetry+benchmark, AC7+DONT2 → KD-5 advisory-only. Snapshot preservation (rq-ctxsnap2 / rq-ctxticker2.5) explicitly called out in Architecture point 1 and KD-2, closing the prior caution. Session-debt binary decision (KD-3) closes the prior ambiguity caution and aligns with AC3. Visibility direction (KD-6) is now explicit: dual-read transition, then converge on registered AdvAffectedProjects matching backlog-coordination precedent — closes the prior 'undeclared Visibility direction' caution. adv_run_test compatibility-first stance (KD-8) and exec-default rationale are corroborated by Node child_process docs (exec spawns shell, supports timeout/maxBuffer/killSignal; spawn defaults shell:false, so non-trivial to swap). Measurement-first + telemetry-first sequencing (Phases A→C with C2 telemetry preceding C3 subprocess changes) respects DONT6.

validation.notes: VALIDATED. No conflicts found across the four validation dimensions. Minor follow-up notes (non-blocking): (a) KD-6 dual-read transition should land tests proving Visibility ID completeness before any summary fast path relies on Visibility-only ID set — design already states this; planning task should make it explicit; (b) KD-3 binary decision must occur before Phase A code lands (design states this); planning gate needs a task that records the remove/confine outcome with evidence; (c) Phase D benchmark repair should document whether bench harness lives under plugin/scripts/ (Temporal-only contract) — design says 'or documented isolated benchmark substitute', acceptable; (d) C7/DONT6 + KD-8 are fully consistent with Node docs — no exec→spawn drop-in unless tests prove shell-metachar/pipe/redirect/timeout/maxBuffer/exit-code parity; (e) Telemetry recording inside safeExecute must avoid creating new mutation paths from read tools (C5/DONT cluster) — adding in-memory counters with bounded windows is safe per existing pattern; (f) Acceptance evidence for AC8 should call out which per-tool stats schema is emitted in health view to keep CI structural-test surface deterministic.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Verdict: VALIDATED. Approve design and proceed to planning. Planning should: (1) add explicit session-debt remove/confine decision task gated before Phase A; (2) sequence Phase A status refactor with RED tests asserting provider non-invocation, slim formatted output, AND required _contextSnapshot emission per rq-ctxsnap2/rq-ctxticker2.5; (3) sequence Phase B telemetry before Phase C subprocess profiling so adv_run_test substep numbers exist before any structural change; (4) keep Phase C compatibility-first — no exec→spawn swap without explicit tests covering shell metacharacters, pipelines, redirects, timeout+killSignal, maxBuffer truncation/termination, exit-code/error.signal classification; (5) for Phase E summary listing surface, add tests that gates/archive/recovery/claim/task-completion paths still call full hydration (KD-5/DONT2); (6) for Phase F Visibility convergence, add transition tests proving AdvAffectedProjects coverage before removing AdvProjectId reliance or explicitly register+document AdvProjectId.
follow_ups: Planning task: session-debt binary decision (remove vs confine) recorded with evidence before Phase A.
Planning task: Phase A RED tests include _contextSnapshot preservation per rq-ctxsnap2 + rq-ctxticker2.5.
Planning task: adv_run_test compatibility test matrix (shell metachars, pipelines, redirects, timeout/killSignal, maxBuffer, exit-code/signal classification) before any subprocess strategy change.
Planning task: telemetry schema for per-tool duration stats nailed down for health view to keep structural CI deterministic.
Planning task: Visibility dual-read → AdvAffectedProjects convergence guarded by tests proving ID coverage.

wisdom.content: For ADV status latency work, OpenCode session-debt diagnostics are valuable as local DB/session archaeology but should not be part of summary or normal health hot paths. Confine expensive session-debt scans to hygiene/explicit doctor surfaces with freshness/degraded metadata; summary should not call scanOpenCodeSessionDebt.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal via inline Tier A approval: "continue". Problem statement confirmed; proposal persisted with scope, success criteria, constraints, failure handling, and discovery agenda.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery re-entered for user-requested adv_run_test hot-path scope. Proposal/agreement updated; revised acceptance criteria approved by user reply `approve`; contract re-minted from revised agreement.

design.approval_evidence: Design persisted for revised scope including adv_run_test hot path. Independent validator verdict: VALIDATED. Prior cautions resolved: _contextSnapshot preservation, session-debt binary decision, explicit Visibility direction.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep task graph with reply `approve`. Task graph includes 9 tasks covering AC1-AC11 with dependencies and contract refs; worktree /home/jon/.local/share/opencode/worktree/bdf259aa162ae192af5b18899ccdc653b085528d/change/improveAdvLatency is active.

execution.approval_evidence: All 9 tasks complete; pnpm run check + full vitest suite pass; manual latency benchmark captured; contract review matrix recorded 25/25 (AC1-AC11 pass, all constraints respected, all avoidances respected); adv_change_validate strict passes (only expected NO_DELTAS warning).

acceptance.approval_evidence: Independent review verdict: READY (0 blocking findings, 2 nonblocking nits). CI: pnpm run check passes. Contract review matrix: 25/25. adv_change_validate strict: pass. Manual benchmark confirms ~2.7x summary-vs-health latency win. Executive summary persisted.

reentry_history.reason: User added `adv_run_test` latency optimization as additional warm-path scope after discovery approval, changing objectives/acceptance coverage before design completion.
reentry_history.scope_delta: Include `adv_run_test` latency/throughput improvement investigation and design alongside warm default read tools, without weakening TDD evidence recording or test execution correctness.

items.text: `adv_status view:"summary"` does not invoke detailed-only health, hygiene, session-debt, snapshot-health, plugin-runtime, project-metadata, or worktree-cleanup providers.
Summary formatted output is slim; health/worktree/session-debt details appear only in detailed views unless audit proves a summary-critical need.
Session-debt diagnostic is audited; remove it if low-value, or confine it to detailed diagnostics.
Warm default reads are optimized first: `adv_status`, `adv_change_list`, and common phase-start reads.
Default list/status paths avoid per-change full hydration when summary data satisfies response contract.
`adv_run_test` hot path is profiled and improved where safe; tests prove task validation, timeout/max-buffer classification, output shaping, and exit-code semantics remain intact.
Summary/cache/test-tool optimizations never authorize gates, archive, worker-lock recovery, claims, task completion, or fabricated test evidence.
Telemetry exposes per-tool `duration_ms`, named `adv_status` phase durations, and enough `adv_run_test` timing/substep attribution to diagnose overhead around command execution.
Benchmark harness works under current Temporal-only store contract and documents command, fixture, output.
CI uses structural regression tests for lazy/provider non-invocation and no-unneeded hydration; manual Temporal benchmark supplies real latency evidence.
Existing spec-law, Temporal cache-refresh, worktree safety, TDD evidence, and gate-completion tests do not regress.
Do not replace Temporal as durable execution/state backend.
Do not remove or weaken Zod validation or schema/boundary correctness for speed.
Do not change gate semantics, approval rules, archive rules, task completion rules, or TDD evidence semantics except documented read/test-tool performance internals that preserve behavior.
Do not let stale cached diagnostics authorize safety-critical mutations or decisions.
Do not create hidden background mutations from read-only tools.
Detailed views/includes must remain able to surface diagnostic data that still has value.
`adv_run_test` must not skip task validation, command execution, timeout enforcement, max-buffer enforcement, or output/error classification.
Tests must respect repo conventions: commands from `plugin/`, Node/Vitest tests, Bun runtime gotchas.
Do not optimize by skipping validation, hiding diagnostics, or fabricating summary/test evidence fields.
Do not make `ChangeSummaryMemo` or any cache a gate/archive/claim/task-evidence authority.
Do not keep expensive summary work only because it is currently present in `formatted`; audit and justify each summary field.
Do not add flaky wall-clock CI budgets as the primary regression guard.
Do not perform broad unrelated status/WIP cleanup beyond latency-driven read/test-path changes.
Do not change exact shell-command semantics for `adv_run_test` unless design evidence proves the replacement preserves compatibility.

rows.evidence: plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts: summary view does not invoke detailed-only providers; pnpm test passes.
plugin/src/tools/status.ts blanks detailed formatted sections in summary view; plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts asserts summary formatted sections are empty; pnpm test passes.
Session-debt confined to hygiene view; plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts 'confines OpenCode session debt to hygiene view' asserts confinement; summary recommendation removed.
Default adv_change_list wired through listSummary (plugin/src/tools/change.ts); status summary skips detailed providers; manual benchmark shows summary ≈42ms vs health ≈115ms.

plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.test.ts 'serves memo-only candidates without per-change full hydration' passes; Visibility list query converged on AdvAffectedProjects in list-change-workflows.ts and visibility-claim-queries.ts.

plugin/src/tools/test.test.ts shell-compatibility matrix (pipe semantics, timeout SIGTERM classification, max-buffer classification, non-zero exit, fresh execution) passes; substep telemetry recorded for adv_run_test.
listSummary defers to authoritative path for archived/closed/content filters; adv_change_close/closeBatch + adv_gate_complete unchanged; adv_task_checkpoint still records evidence on done.

plugin/src/utils/metrics.test.ts asserts recordToolDuration and recordPhaseDuration semantics; plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts 'records named adv_status phase durations' passes; plugin/src/tools/test.test.ts 'records adv_run_test substep telemetry phases' passes.
plugin/scripts/bench-adv-latency.ts rewritten with disk substitute; smoke run captured at reports/latency-disk-final.md; docs/bench-adv-latency.md documents command/fixture/output.

CI structural tests in plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts (provider non-invocation, slim formatted sections) and plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.test.ts (no per-change hydration); manual Temporal benchmark via bench-adv-latency.ts mode=temporal documented.
Full `pnpm test` suite passes; pnpm run check passes; no spec-law/cache-refresh/worktree/TDD/gate test regressed.
Temporal store backend unchanged; createStore still requires temporalBundle; visibility filter switched but stays on registered Temporal search attribute.
No Zod schema removed; preflight/validator paths untouched.
Gate completion, archive, task checkpoint, TDD evidence semantics unchanged; adv_run_test contract preserved.
listSummary explicitly hands off to authoritative listResolvedChanges for archived/closed/content-filter callers; cached metrics expose data via read-only health view.
advWorktreeCleanup removed from summary; no read-only tool mutates state. Bench writes only to its own XDG_DATA_HOME temp dir.
Detailed views still expose temporal_health, worker diagnostics, session debt, snapshot health, plugin runtime, project metadata, external state hygiene.
adv_run_test still validates taskId, runs exec with timeout/maxBuffer, classifies failures, and shapes output. Shell compatibility tests cover semantics.
All tests run via `pnpm test` from plugin/; Vitest-compatible imports; benchmark uses Node/Bun compatible APIs only.
Validation untouched; diagnostics moved between views with explicit audit; no fabricated fields added.
ChangeSummaryMemo remains advisory; authoritative paths used for terminal/safety-critical callers.
Summary plan documents per-field audit; session-debt removed from summary based on user-validated value; OpenCode debt retained only in hygiene.
CI relies on structural tests (provider non-invocation, no-unneeded hydration, slim formatted sections); manual benchmark provides wall-clock evidence.
Only files in scope (status/test/change/metrics/visibility/store/specs) modified plus benchmark/docs additions; no unrelated WIP cleanup.
adv_run_test still uses child_process.exec with original options; compatibility matrix tests cover preserved semantics.

acceptanceCriteria: `adv_status view:"summary"` does not invoke detailed-only health, hygiene, session-debt, snapshot-health, plugin-runtime, project-metadata, or worktree-cleanup providers.
Summary formatted output is slim; health/worktree/session-debt details appear only in detailed views unless audit proves a summary-critical need.
Session-debt diagnostic is audited; remove it if low-value, or confine it to detailed diagnostics.
Warm default reads are optimized first: `adv_status`, `adv_change_list`, and common phase-start reads.
Default list/status paths avoid per-change full hydration when summary data satisfies response contract.
`adv_run_test` hot path is profiled and improved where safe; tests prove task validation, timeout/max-buffer classification, output shaping, and exit-code semantics remain intact.
Summary/cache/test-tool optimizations never authorize gates, archive, worker-lock recovery, claims, task completion, or fabricated test evidence.
Telemetry exposes per-tool `duration_ms`, named `adv_status` phase durations, and enough `adv_run_test` timing/substep attribution to diagnose overhead around command execution.
Benchmark harness works under current Temporal-only store contract and documents command, fixture, output.
CI uses structural regression tests for lazy/provider non-invocation and no-unneeded hydration; manual Temporal benchmark supplies real latency evidence.
Existing spec-law, Temporal cache-refresh, worktree safety, TDD evidence, and gate-completion tests do not regress.

clarify_findings.message: Success criteria are placeholder or empty. What measurable outcomes define done?
Proposal has no Scope section. What files and modules will be affected?
